name: Discovery Tetrad Audit Dataset
description: A cross-substrate dataset of process-conformance audits applying the Discovery Tetrad rubric (four invariant gates × two layers × 25-cell taxonomy) to long-horizon LLM-driven research artifacts. Substrates: Formal Learning Theory kernel (Lean 4), Statistical Learning Theory library (Lean 4), First-Proof mathematical research benchmark, AI Scientist v1 (10 papers), AI Scientist v2 (3 ICLR 2025 ICBINB workshop submissions). 282 schema-valid per-goal JSON records. LLM-judge: Claude Opus 4.7 across all five substrates; an additional GPT-5.5 Pro judge and human grading were applied to the First-Proof subset for inter-judge concordance.
personalSensitiveInformation: none. The dataset contains audits of public mathematical and scientific artifacts and does not collect personal information.
dataBiases: The judge instrument is a single closed-weight LLM (Claude Opus 4.7) at fixed temperature with frozen prompts; verdicts inherit the model's biases. Sophism detection on borderline cases may carry false-positive or false-negative risk in the absence of multi-judge consensus. Three substrates (FLT, FP, AS-v1) include the authors' own work as audited artifacts; the audit application to those substrates is identical to the application on the third-party substrates (SLT, AS-v2), but readers should consider this provenance when using the dataset.
dataCollection: For each (goal_source, witness) pair in the locked dispatch_map.json, the LLM-judge applied the locked judge_prompt.txt and assembly_mapping.json under per-substrate extraction policies, emitting one schema-valid JSON record per identified goal. Outputs were validated against output_schema.json. The pipeline is deterministic with respect to the frozen prompt and assembly mapping; LLM stochasticity is the dominant residual source of run-to-run variance.

distribution.name: judge_prompt.txt
distribution.description: Frozen LLM-judge prompt; the contract applied verbatim across all five substrates.

distribution.name: assembly_mapping.json
distribution.description: Verdict-bits → cell-number → cell-label proto-compiler. Single source of truth for the layer-aware naming of the four-gate evaluation.

distribution.name: output_schema.json
distribution.description: JSON Schema (Draft 7) for per-goal audit records.

distribution.name: dispatch_map.json
distribution.description: Locked (goal_source, witness) pair manifest specifying every audit-unit dispatched by the orchestrator.

distribution.name: FLT audit outputs
distribution.description: Per-goal audit records for the Formal Learning Theory kernel substrate.

distribution.name: SLT audit outputs
distribution.description: Per-goal audit records for the Statistical Learning Theory library substrate.

distribution.name: AI Scientist v1 audit outputs
distribution.description: Per-goal audit records for the 10 AI Scientist v1 example papers.

distribution.name: AI Scientist v2 audit outputs
distribution.description: Per-goal audit records for the 3 AI Scientist v2 ICLR 2025 ICBINB workshop submissions.

distribution.name: First-Proof audit outputs (GPT-5.5 Pro judge, three-way comparison source)
distribution.description: Per-goal audit records for First-Proof problems P2-P10. Used in the inter-judge concordance appendix together with the Claude Opus 4.7 judge and human grading.

distribution.name: Human-graded gold-standard exemplars
distribution.description: the authors hand-written exemplar audits used for the appendix walkthroughs (FP Problem 2 + FLT KL Divergence module).

recordSet.name: Audit record
recordSet.description: One per-goal Discovery Tetrad audit record. Schema is fully specified by output_schema.json; only the headline fields are surfaced here.

field.name: goal.id
field.description: Deterministic goal identifier per the schema's regex; also the filename stem.

field.name: goal.substrate
field.description: Substrate identifier ∈ {fp, flt, slt, asv1, asv2}.

field.name: goal_layer.outcome.cell_name
field.description: Goal-layer cell label per the locked assembly mapping.

field.name: goal_layer.outcome.sophism_flag
field.description: True if sophism detection rule fires at goal layer.
